title: Feed Discovery, URL Identity, Scope, and SSRF Boundary · Go Build Room
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds authorized feed discovery and network-scope policy from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of authorized feed discovery and network-scope policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, cha…
objectives: Distinguish a direct feed URL from HTML autodiscovery and require an explicit bounded discovery policy.
Admit only alternate links with supported RSS or Atom media types, resolve references against the response URL, and bound candidates.
Normalize only justified URI components while preserving distinctions that can identify different publisher resources.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Direct Vs Discovery
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 4973 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile bounded feed discovery, URL identity, SSRF decisions, and provenance; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Direct Vs Discovery.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish a direct feed url from html autodiscovery and require an explicit bounded discovery policy. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Direct Vs Discovery, keep Feed Go Direct Vs Discovery connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
